title: The Next One
description: The party stands at the portal in Dr. Temporal's workshop. They reckon with the diagram — a map of phases, not an administrative chart. Five more worlds like this one. The Fighter steps through first.

lines: The portal hummed in the corner of Dr. Temporal's workshop. It showed parking lots. Picket fences. A world that made no architectural sense next to the one they were standing in.
Nobody had looked away from the diagram yet. The one they'd thought was an administrative chart.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: The diagram — the administrative chart — it wasn't administrative at all. It was a map of phases. And the scholar said there were five more like it.

steps.text: The Fighter stepped through first. He always did. Not because he wasn't afraid. Because someone had to, and he had decided a long time ago that the someone would be him.

lines: The medieval world didn't end behind them — it simply stopped being relevant. Like a chapter in a book no one was reading anymore.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: The combat scripts — they came through. Different syntax, same logic. The automation translated.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: We didn't miss anything back there, did we? I keep feeling like we left something unfinished. Phil said something about dreams — metal walls, blinking lights. I wrote it all down.
